title: polanisia uniglandulosa
intro: Polanisia uniglandulosa, also known as Clasping-leaf Spiderwort, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the southeastern United States. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, as well as its ability to stimulate the immune system and reduce fever. The health benefits of Polanisia uniglandulosa include its potential to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, such as sore throats and coughs, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or tincture, and it is also used in herbal remedies in the form of infusions, salves, and poultices.
